---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

# Tables

By default, R Markdown displays data frames and matrixes as they would be in the R terminal (in a monospaced font). If you prefer that data be displayed with additional formatting you can use the `knitr::kable` function, as in the .Rmd file below. This file is available [here](https://posit.cloud/content/181946) on RStudio Cloud.

![](https://d33wubrfki0l68.cloudfront.net/09467251a219c3c6b2dae2bf1367e5736a9ef78c/feeea/lesson-images/tables-1-kable.png)

Note the use of the `results='asis'` chunk option. This is required to ensure that the raw table output isn’t processed further by knitr.
